author: @petermoll3421
text:
Thanks for your reply Rob,

I suppose I'm a bit jaded by all the snake oil sellers out there in the quest for over unity.

I guess i was hoping you would do as you have done in the past and validate the experiment with some real data.

There were a number of airflow sources that came to mind;
compressed air,
an inflator such as you used, 
and last but not least my favored method, numerous cartons of beer for me to generate an airflow etc.( the wife won't like the last one and the impact on the environment might be disastrous)

And I'm sure there would be others but all of these would require an input of energy which is greater than the output, if the laws of physics are correct.

Call me a naysayer if you will but is it not just a different method of coupling a power source to a generator, of which there are many different types with varying efficiencies, and if so what is the advantage.

Tesla claimed an efficiency of 97% but to date as far as I can read only 40% or so has been achieved, much less than conventional turbines.

With regard to the solar cells and wind turbine, the reason I use them is because the power source is there and freely available to all, at this point until the governments find a way to tax them, with minimal impact on the environment.

And if the light wasn't there I think we would all be in a bit of a pickle. A bit of a moot point.

I apologize if my asking of pertinent questions makes you think I'm being negative, but I think it would be rather ignorant of me if I didn't ask questions. If you feel that they were irrelevant and you don't want to answer those questions please just say so. 

Sincerely
Peter

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: +PETER MOLL no worries mate and it is a question of time - i will provide data in time - but we aren't on a 'free energy' quest here - have a look at the on efficiency video i just did this should help understand my viewpoint

author: @petermoll3421
text:
Probably going to stir up an ants nest, but anyway.

How much energy was expended to produce the electricity?
Where in the practical world would you source the air flow to run the turbine?
Is this more efficient than other ways of producing power e.g. solar, wind or even an ICE?

Living in an off grid situation I have looked at many alternative ways of producing power and practicality has drawn me back to solar panels for main production with a wind turbine to supplement them and the obligatory battery bank. A petrol generator for the system backup and for using heavy draw equipment such as a MIG welder and large air compressor.

The only part of the system that I see could be changed would be the batteries in favor of your capacitors.

I apologize if I offend anyone but I can only this as a toy, and that is well and good if it inspires people to experiment.

Waiting for the flaming.

Peter

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text:
+PETER MOLL i don't think what you are saying is that controversial mate. In my mind it's a bit of fun and inspires experiment - what more reason do you need really?
Your questions aren't that problematic i can think of a few ways to source the airflow required and quite a few practical applications - but i think that is a problem for experimenters to solve - i'll probably share a few if i build them.
Solar cells are only more efficient for you because you don't have to generate the light - if you did they would only be around 7% efficient - you'd probably throw them in the bin if did have to generate the light lol.
It might be a better use of your time if you spent a little time thinking how they could be used instead of finding reasons they can't. If you do that I think you'll hit on the difference between the average person and the inventor

author: @ravenbishop2557
text: i think it would generate more power, if you switched the input to the central port currently used for exhaust, because centrifugal force is going to make the air volume have the tendency to move to the outside, working with your design instead of against it...this may require some means of getting the platters spinning (smaller starter tesla turbine would work well i think) but this is fantastic on its own :) thank you.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: +raven bishop I don't think it would work that way mate as you are using the flow of air to turn the disks rather than spinning the disks to move the air

replies.author: @ravenbishop2557
replies.text: +Robert Murray-Smith  i think you can, if you offset the holes on the disks by half the radius of the holes it should spin in the direction of the offset.

replies.author: @ravenbishop2557
replies.text: +raven bishop or against the direction of the offset depending on how you look at it :)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: +raven bishop I see what you mean and it's pretty easy to try - so - i'll give it a go - cheers mate oh and by the way i have no problem being wrong lol

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: +raven bishop I see what you mean and it's pretty easy to try - so - i'll give it a go - cheers mate oh and by the way i have no problem being wrong lol
